title: Nuclei Extraction from Flash-Frozen Grafted Mouse Brains for 10X Genomics Single-Cell Sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9qcrb","text":"This protocol explains how to extract and process nuclei from the dorsal striatum of flash-frozen mouse brains containing grafts for single-nucleus RNA-sequencing using 10X Genomics technology.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"egtj3","text":"All working surfaces and equipment have to be treated and handled for RNA extraction, for example, cleaned with RNAse-away for RNAse decontamination and RNAse-free disposable elements like the eppendorf tubes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"12kne","text":"Remember to check your local policies when working with human samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"79ot9","text":"In our case, our fresh human cells had to be worked in BSL2-compliant rooms.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

No. 1000494)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dn4n7","text":"10X Genomics Chromium GEM-X Single Cell 3' Kit v4 (1000691)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7etlc","text":"Chromium GEM-X Single Cell 3' Chip Kit v4 (1000690)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"57l9p","text":"Dual Index Kit TT Set A (1000215)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"56tsd","text":"10X Genomics recommedend LoBind Eppendorf tubes (2 mL, 0.5 mL)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ebdtc","text":"1% Nuclease-free BSA solution","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4o4on","text":"Sterile filtered PBS 1X","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Euthanasia : Euthanize animals via cervical dislocation.
Brain Extraction : Promptly extract the whole brain immediately after confirmed death, using scissors and forceps to break open the skull, and spatula to remove the brain.
Flash-Freezing : Submerge brains in dry-ice chilled isopentane for 10–15 seconds to flash-freeze.
Storage : Transfer flash-frozen brains to -80°C for storage until further processing. Liquid nitrogen works for very long term storage.
Use scissors or scalpels to open the skin and expose skull
Use the blunt forceps to lift the detached skull, exposing the brain
Use the scissors or bone rongeurs to cut the skull between the eyes, and into the ears from the back
Remove the brain using a spoon or a spatula by slowly detaching the nerves from the brain.
Equilibration : Equilibrate brains to -20°C before slicing.
Sectioning Setup : Place brains in a - 20°C cooled slicing chamber . Stabilize using a brain matrix.
Slicing : Slice brains into four 1-mm thick coronal sections. 2 slices anterior and 2 slices posterior to the center of the graft implantation site . Use chilled razor blades for clean cuts.
Tissue Punching : Using 1 mm biopsy punchers, punch dorsal striatum punches (where graft is expected) from each slice. Collect punches into pre-prepared tubes (see below, step 9).
Prepare 1% Nuclease-free BSA in sterile nuclease-free PBS1X
Pre-coat 2 and 0.5 mL LoBind Eppendorf tubes with 1% BSA overnight at 4°C .
Before use, briefly rinse tubes with sterile-filtered 1X PBS.
Perform nuclei isolation using the 10X Genomics Chromium Nuclei Isolation Kit (1000494). Follow the manufacturer’s instructions for optimal results.
It is recommended to continue the GEM preparation inmediately
creator: Roberto Garcia Swinburn
